calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2015 Continued To Jun-24-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Thad Davis, Sarah Zenewicz, And Ropes Gray Llps Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pursuant To C.C.P., Â§ 425.16 [Anti-Slapp Motion]
Off Calendar May-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN MCDONNELL, TEL. NO. (650) 991-9909 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ALLISON LANE OF DUANE MORRIS, TEL. NO. 957-3045 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THAD DAVIS, SARAH ZENEWICZ AND ROPES & GRAY LLP'S Motion To Strike Pursuant To C.C.P., Sec. 425.16 [Anti-Slapp Motion] is Granted. The anti-SLAPP motion is timely filed. CCP sec. 425.16(f) provides that an anti-SLAPP motion "may be filed within 60 days of the service of the complaint'" A defendant can waive service by making a general appearance. (CCP sec. 410.50(a).) A motion to strike constitutes a general appearance. (CCP sec. 1014.) Accordingly, "service" and the filing of the anti-SLAPP motion effectively occurred simultaneously. Additionally, the use of the word "within" indicates that the 60 day requirement sets forth the outer limit for filing the motion and does not "fix the first point in time." (Glass v. Benkert (1971) 18 Cal.App.3d 322, 327.) Lastly, the purpose of the anti-SLAPP statute is to "enable the defendant-victim of a SLAPP suit to extricate himself or herself from the lawsuit as quickly and inexplicably as possible." (S.B. Beach Properties v. Berti (2006) 39 Cal.4th 374, 382.) Requiring a defendant to wait until the complaint is served on him to file an anti-SLAPP motion would run afoul to this policy. Because the motion was timely filed, the Court reaches the merits of the motion. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity'If the court finds such a showing has been made'it then determines whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) The first prong is met if the moving party meets the burden of making a prima facie showing that the plaintiff's cause of action arises out of an act in furtherance of a person's right to petition or free speech under the U.S. or California Constitutions in connection with a public issue. (CCP 425.16(b)(1).) The entire complaint arises from Defendants' right to petition on behalf of their clients and therefore the first prong is met here. (See Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 479-80.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated a probability of prevailing and therefore the motion is granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/hk
